critique.summary: The article describes Fortransky, a terminal-only Bluesky / AT Protocol client written primarily in Fortran, with a Rust native firehose decoder for handling the relay-raw stream. It details the architecture, build dependencies, usage instructions, and features of the client.
mainPoints: Fortransky is a terminal user interface client for Bluesky / AT Protocol written in Fortran.
It uses a Rust static library to decode the relay-raw firehose stream, converting binary CBOR data into JSON.
The client supports login via app passwords, session saving, and various commands for timeline, posting, notifications, and streaming.
It can toggle between two stream modes: jetstream (JSON native) and relay-raw (binary CBOR).
The project includes build instructions, dependencies (Fortran compiler, Rust toolchain, Python packages), and known limitations.
The client is open source and hosted on GitHub under the FormerLab organization.
critique.truthfulness: The claims about the Fortransky client are consistent with the publicly available GitHub repository at the provided URL. The repository shows the described architecture, language usage, and features. The technical details about dependencies, build process, and stream handling align with typical implementations for such clients. There are no exaggerated or false claims detected in the description.
considerations: The article does not mention the current stability or maturity level of the client beyond known issues.
No information is provided about user adoption, community support, or security audits of the client.
The performance implications of using Fortran and Rust together for this application are not discussed.
Potential limitations in feature completeness compared to official or other third-party Bluesky clients are not addressed.

comments_critique.summary: The discussion centers around the choice of Fortran for building a terminal-only Bluesky/AT Proto client, with some users expressing curiosity and others making brief comparative remarks.

comments.author: cat-turner
comments.text: out of curiosity, why fortran? no disrespect. I wrote a lot of scientific software in the earlier days of my career and I learned fortran to update ocean modeling software.
comments.analysis: This comment raises a relevant and thoughtful question about the choice of programming language, reflecting personal experience and genuine curiosity.
